headline: Large User Grid Cost Responsibility
short_summary: This bill shifts the financial burden of grid infrastructure from residential ratepayers to large energy users, ensuring corporations pay for their own energy demands.
status: Last Action: Signed by President on 2026-04-22
supporters: Residential consumer advocacy groups, environmental organizations.
opponents: Data center industry, large industrial energy users.
news_headline: Governor Amends Energy Bills, Shifts Data Center Costs
news_summary: Governor Spanberger amended HB 1393, removing a direct cost shift for PJM power but allowing the SCC to ensure data centers pay their fair share. These changes are expected to save residential customers an average of $5.52 per month.

headline: Data Center Water Use Transparency
short_summary: This legislation mandates data centers to report their water consumption, providing transparency on their environmental impact.
status: Last Action: Approved by Governor-Chapter 896 (Effective 1/1/2027) on 2026-04-13
supporters: Environmental groups, local governments, water conservation advocates.
opponents: Data center industry (potentially due to reporting burden).
news_headline: Virginia Bill Increases Data Center Water Reporting
news_summary: Senate Bill 553, which was awaiting the governor's signature, aims to increase transparency by requiring data centers to report their water consumption. Senator Kannan Srinivasan stated this bill would bring much-needed transparency to water use.

headline: Data Center Location and Noise Rules
short_summary: This bill establishes guidelines for where data centers can be built and limits the noise they produce, addressing community concerns.
status: Last Action: Incorporated by Counties, Cities and Towns (HB153-Thomas) (Voice Vote) on 2026-01-23
supporters: Local residents, community groups, local governments.
opponents: Data center developers, real estate industry.
news_headline: Virginia Bill Mandates Data Center Siting Assessments
news_summary: A bill, supported by Del. Joshua Thomas, requires site assessments for new data centers, including noise studies near homes and schools. This legislation addresses community concerns about data center proximity and environmental impacts.
